AC:
1. GET/POST http://esp32s3-office:5000/dmz/ping performs one signed DMZ sensors POST.
2. Onboard logs include DMZ status and a useful excerpt of the response body.
3. Endpoint JSON reports ok/status/body_excerpt (or clear error if sign/time/network fails).
4. No unsigned POSTs; uses auth.toit Ed25519 path.
ActualSummary: Committed /dmz/ping Ed25519 path (main.toit/auth/secrets deploy), Midea IR TX, and office test fixes. Deployed to esp32s3-office; /dmz/ping returns status/body_excerpt and logs DMZ response. Live DMZ auth still 401 until board NTP (epoch ~745k).

Body.raw:
Child of 21e3454c (local /healthz /logs /gpio /ir/ping).

## Goal

Add GET (or POST) /dmz/ping on the office ESP32 debug HTTP server that does
ONE signed roundtrip to the DMZ sensors long-poll endpoint.

## Behavior

1. Build local state body (sensors fallback OK; include deployment config).
2. Sign with Ed25519 (auth.toit / custom-envelope C service) using zone seed
   from gitignored secrets generated at deploy.
3. POST http://jovlinger.duckdns.org:5000/zone/office/sensors with
   X-Zone-Signature, X-Zone-Timestamp, X-Zone-Name (long-poll hold is OK).
4. Log what DMZ sends back (status + body snippet) into the onboard log ring
   so GET /logs and the /dmz/ping JSON response surface it.

## Notes

- Encryption required (uses existing auth library already KAT-passing on device).
- Direct local exercise: curl http://192.168.88.73:5000/dmz/ping
- Parent already serves :5000 healthz/logs/gpio/ir/ping on thermo-esp32s3.

Summary.raw: ESP32 /dmz/ping: signed one-shot DMZ sensor long-poll + log response

WorkItems.summary: Fix office zone tests expecting pico2w (now esp32s3)

WorkItems.message: Deployed thermo-esp32s3 via zones/office; curl /dmz/ping returns ok:false status:401 body_excerpt invalid signature + clock-skew log (epoch unsynced); healthz auth_ed25519_ready true
WorkItems.summary: Deploy to esp32s3-office and curl-verify /dmz/ping AC (signed path + logs)
